context_pointers.vague_reference: follow the standard traversal naming conventions used in the connectivity module
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The output_rule expects specific strings like 'depth_first' or 'breadth_first' which correspond to the mode arguments in traverse.py

context_pointers.vague_reference: use the same naming prefix for the component identifiers as defined in the strong_connectivity utility
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The graphmap_components parser outputs SCCs with prefixes like 'scc_1', 'scc_2' which must be consistent with the conversion logic

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_undirected_graph_mutation.json.description: Undirected graph adjacency, self-loop, parallel edge, and node removal behavior.

input.scenario: remove a node after adding reciprocal, self-loop, and parallel connections

cases.expected_output:
before_nodes=4
before_edges=7
before_has_a_b=yes
before_has_d_a=yes
before_has_a_a=yes
before_neighbors_of_b=[0, 2, 0]
after_nodes=3
after_edges=1
after_has_a_b=no
after_has_d_a=no
after_has_a_a=no
after_has_b_c=yes
after_neighbors_of_b=[2]

feature2_edge_update.json.description: Updating an existing edge reuses the edge slot in the relevant direction instead of creating a duplicate.

input.scenario: compare directed forward updates with undirected reverse-endpoint updates

cases.expected_output:
directed_edges=2
directed_reused_forward_id=yes
directed_forward_weight=2
undirected_edges=1
undirected_reused_reverse_id=yes
undirected_edge_weight=2

feature3_1_traversal_reachability.json.description: Traversal reports the reachable portion of a directed graph and can traverse the reversed view.

input.scenario: reachable counts from several starts including the reversed graph view

cases.expected_output:
walk=depth_first
from_H_count=4
from_H_order=["H", "I", "K", "J"]
from_I_count=3
reverse_from_H_count=1
reverse_from_K_count=3

input.scenario: reachable counts and visit order from the same graph

cases.expected_output:
walk=breadth_first
from_H_count=4
from_H_order=["H", "J", "I", "K"]
from_I_count=3
reverse_from_H_count=1
reverse_from_K_count=3

feature3_2_shortest_paths.json.description: Shortest path algorithms return path costs, reachable labels, and no-path signals for weighted graphs.

input.scenario: all shortest distances and one target-limited query in an undirected weighted graph

cases.expected_output:
algorithm=dijkstra
distances=A:0,B:7,C:9,D:11,E:20,F:20
target_C_distance=9

input.scenario: zero heuristic route and unreachable reverse route in a directed weighted graph

cases.expected_output:
algorithm=astar_zero
A_to_E_cost=23
A_to_E_path=["A", "D", "E"]
E_to_B_reachable=no

input.scenario: coordinate heuristic route in a weighted directed graph

cases.expected_output:
algorithm=astar_manhattan
start_to_goal_cost=6
start_to_goal_path=(0,0)->(0,2)->(3,3)->(4,2)

feature3_3_undirected_cycle_detection.js.description: Undirected cycle detection distinguishes trees from self-loops, triangle closures, and longer cycle closures.

input.scenario: add and remove cycle-forming edges while observing cycle presence

cases.expected_output:
initial=no
after_tree_edges=no
with_self_loop=yes
after_self_loop_removed=no
with_triangle_edge=yes
after_triangle_removed=no
after_path_extension=no
after_closing_long_cycle=yes

feature3_4_minimum_spanning_forest.json.description: A minimum spanning forest keeps all nodes, selects minimum connecting edges per component, and rejects heavier alternatives.

input.scenario: two-component weighted graph with exact selected and rejected edges

cases.expected_output:
nodes=10
edges=8
selected_edges=A-B,A-D,B-E,E-C,E-G,D-F,H-I,I-J
rejected_D_B=yes
rejected_B_C=yes

feature4_1_keyed_weighted_graph.json.description: A keyed undirected graph stores nodes by value, replaces duplicate edge weights, and supports weighted distances.

input.scenario: insert weighted edges, replace duplicates, then compute distances

cases.expected_output:
nodes=6
edges=9
first_E_F_was_new=yes
old_F_B_weight=15
old_F_E_weight=5
current_E_F_weight=6
distances=A:0,B:7,C:9,D:11,E:20,F:20

feature4_2_keyed_edge_removal.json.description: Removing keyed graph edges respects whether the graph is undirected or directed.

input.scenario: remove an undirected edge using reverse endpoint order

cases.expected_output:
direction=undirected
before_1_to_2=-1
before_2_to_1=-1
before_neighbors_of_1=1
remove_missing=missing
remove_existing_reverse=-1
after_edges=0
after_1_to_2=missing
after_2_to_1=missing
after_neighbors_of_1=0

input.scenario: attempt reverse removal before removing the directed edge in forward order

cases.expected_output:
direction=directed
before_1_to_2=-1
before_2_to_1=missing
before_neighbors_of_1=1
remove_missing=missing
remove_reverse=missing
remove_forward=-1
after_edges=0
after_1_to_2=missing
after_2_to_1=missing
after_neighbors_of_1=0

feature4_3_keyed_components_and_conversi.description: A keyed directed graph can report strongly connected components and convert to an indexed graph without losing edge weights.

input.scenario: compute components and convert the keyed graph to indexed form

cases.expected_output:
sccs=[[0, 3, 6], [1, 4, 7], [2, 5, 8]]
converted_nodes=9
converted_edges=11

feature5_1_stable_indices_and_vacancies..description: A stable indexed graph preserves index holes, reports bounds separately from counts, and reuses vacancies.

input.scenario: remove nodes, clear edges, and observe remaining indices and reused vacancies

cases.expected_output:
remaining_node_indices=[0, 2]
node_bound_full=10
node_bound_after_removals=10
node_bound_after_clear=0
reused_after_clear_edges=[1,9]
edge_count_after_clear_edges=0

feature5_2_stable_strong_components.json.description: Strongly connected component algorithms work on stable indexed directed graphs with removed nodes.

input.scenario: component groups after replacing and removing one node

cases.expected_output:
algorithm=kosaraju
components=[[0, 3, 6], [1, 7, 9], [2, 5, 8]]

input.scenario: component groups after replacing and removing one node

cases.expected_output:
algorithm=tarjan
components=[[0, 3, 6], [1, 7, 9], [2, 5, 8]]

feature5_3_stable_dot_output.json.description: A stable indexed graph renders node labels, self-loops, and directed edges in DOT text.

input.scenario: two labeled nodes with one self-loop and one directed edge

feature5_4_stable_invalid_endpoint_error.description: Adding an edge to a missing stable index is reported as a normalized invalid-reference error.

input.scenario: target index was previously removed

cases.expected_output: error=invalid_graph_reference

input.scenario: target index is beyond the current graph bound

cases.expected_output: error=invalid_graph_reference

feature6_disjoint_set_union.json.description: Disjoint-set union reports initial representatives, merge relationships, set counts, and full labeling collapse.

input.scenario: merge several sets and convert a larger connected structure into labels

cases.expected_output:
initial_roots=0:0:yes,1:1:yes,2:2:yes,3:3:yes,4:4:yes,5:5:yes,6:6:yes,7:7:yes
same_0_1=yes
same_0_3=yes
same_1_3=yes
different_0_2=yes
same_7_0=yes
same_6_5=yes
different_6_7=yes
set_count=3
labeling_single_component=yes

feature7_1_graph_isomorphism.json.description: Graph isomorphism depends on structure rather than concrete node names and changes as nodes or edges are added.

input.scenario: incremental
input.graph_pair: incrementally add equal and unequal node or edge structure

cases.expected_output:
scenario=incremental
empty=yes
one_node_each=yes
two_nodes_each=yes
extra_node_left=no
three_nodes_each=yes
edge_left_only=no
matching_edges=yes

input.scenario: renamed_shape
input.graph_pair: same directed shape with renamed nodes and mirrored construction order

cases.expected_output:
scenario=renamed_shape
one_edge=yes
two_edges=yes
three_cycle=yes
after_two_isolated_nodes=yes
after_renamed_tail=yes

feature7_2_weight_sensitive_isomorphism..description: Weighted isomorphism with custom matching rejects graphs whose edge weights differ.

input.scenario: change individual edge weights while requiring equal node and edge weights

cases.expected_output:
edge0_weight_changed=no
edge1_weight_changed=no

_constraints.input_condition: Add reciprocal, self-loop, parallel connections, then remove a specific node.
_constraints.output_rule: Report node count, edge count, existence checks ('has_X_Y'), adjacency list ('neighbors_of_X'), and post-removal state.

_constraints.input_condition: Attempt to update an edge that already exists in the graph.
_constraints.output_rule: Verify that the edge is reused rather than creating a duplicate (parallel edges).

_constraints.input_condition: Run DFS or BFS traversal on a directed graph from one or more start nodes, including the reversed graph view.
_constraints.output_rule: Report the count of reachable nodes and the specific visit order for each start.

_constraints.input_condition: Run Dijkstra's algorithm on a weighted, undirected graph.
_constraints.output_rule: Return a dictionary of distances for all reachable nodes and the specific distance to a chosen target.

_constraints.input_condition: Run A* search with a zero heuristic on a directed, weighted graph.
_constraints.output_rule: Return cost, path sequence, and reachability status for specific source-destination pairs.

_constraints.input_condition: Run A* search with Manhattan distance heuristic on a directed, weighted graph.
_constraints.output_rule: Return cost and path sequence representing the route.

_constraints.input_condition: Add or remove edges that form cycles (self-loops, triangles, longer paths) in an undirected graph.
_constraints.output_rule: Report binary presence ('yes'/'no') of cycles before and after operations.

_constraints.input_condition: Parse a weighted graph with multiple connected components.
_constraints.output_rule: Report total node count, total edge count, list of selected edges, and list of rejected edges.

_constraints.input_condition: Insert duplicate weighted edges into a keyed undirected graph.
_constraints.output_rule: The last insertion replaces the previous weight. Report old weights, new weight, and distance calculations.

_constraints.input_condition: Attempt to remove an edge using a reversed endpoint order in an undirected graph.
_constraints.output_rule: Successful removal using reverse order removes the edge shared by both directions.

_constraints.input_condition: Attempt to remove an edge using a reversed endpoint order in a directed graph.
_constraints.output_rule: Removal fails if the specific directed order is not matched. Only forward removal works.

_constraints.input_condition: Analyze strong connectivity in a keyed graph and convert it to an indexed representation.
_constraints.output_rule: Report groups of nodes in each SCC and the counts of nodes/edges in the converted indexed form.

_constraints.input_condition: Remove nodes from a stable indexed graph, then clear edges.
_constraints.output_rule: Report persistent index holes, maximum bounds before/after operations, and valid values reused after edge clear.

_constraints.input_condition: Run SCC algorithms (Kosaraju or Tarjan) on a stable indexed graph with removed nodes.
_constraints.output_rule: Components must contain only existing/remaining node identifiers.

_constraints.input_condition: Generate DOT graph output for a stable indexed graph.
_constraints.output_rule: Output must be valid DOT syntax text.

_constraints.input_condition: Attempt to create an edge involving a removed index or an out-of-bounds index.
Merge sets and query relationships between arbitrary points.
_constraints.output_rule: Report initial roots, merged pair relations, set counts, and labeling status.

_constraints.input_condition: Incrementally add nodes and edges to isomorphic or non-isomorphic graphs.
_constraints.output_rule: Report boolean equality ('yes'/'no') for structural equivalence at each step.

_constraints.input_condition: Compare graphs with the same structure but renamed nodes and reversed construction order.
_constraints.output_rule: Report boolean equality ('yes'/'no') for structural equivalence.

_constraints.input_condition: Compare weighted graphs where one has a changed edge weight.
